items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate's renewed defamation claim names Australian YouTuber

children.content: An Adelaide YouTuber has been identified in a renewed defamation lawsuit self-described misogynist Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have lodged with a US court against some of their online critics.

items.title: Blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

children.content: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

image.altText: a man sits in a reclinable chair with a bandage on his inner elbow and he squeezes a stress ball

items.title: Octopus species mysteriously disappears from Spencer Gulf

children.content: The once-abundant pale octopus has not been caught in lower Spencer Gulf for more than a year, and what happened to the species remains a mystery.

image.altText: An orange octopus on a blue fishing bin lip

items.title: 'No rush': Crows won't put timeline on Dawson's return to play

children.content: Adelaide coach Matthew Nicks says there will be no rush for captain Jordan Dawson to return to playing after the death of his brother last week.

articles.title: Crows sensationally down Saints after emotional scenes at Adelaide Oval

children.content: The Crows hold on by one point in a thriller at Adelaide Oval, as the club pays tribute to grieving skipper Jordan Dawson.

items.title: A blunder in Narungga leaves SA's electoral authority in damage control

children.content: The 2026 state election has been a reputational disaster for South Australia's electoral authority. Its blunder in Narungga was the "cherry on top".

image.altText: A hand puts a lower house vote into a sealed ballot box as another person points to the top of the box

items.title: LIV Golf facing fight to 'keep going', CEO admits in deleted interview

children.content: The future of LIV Golf remains in doubt as chief executive Scott O'Neil admits in an interview that the league is "funded through the season" but work is required beyond that "to keep us going".

image.altText: Scott O'Neil walks on the first tee box in front of LIV Golf signage

items.title: 'High-octane world' of drifting told through music in new concerto

children.content: Motorsport and orchestral music are coming together in a premiere in Adelaide this weekend.

image.altText: A clarinetist rehearsing a concerto.

items.title: Fishers deploy 'ninja tactics' to outsmart fish-stealing seals

children.content: Commercial fishers question their future as they compete with ravenous long-nosed seals that continue to rip man-sized holes in fish nets in South Australia's Coorong.

image.altText: A close up of the face of a wet seal resting on a log

items.title: This type of croquet is a far cry from the Alice in Wonderland version

items.title: BreastScreen SA did not get ethics approval or consent before research, ombudsman finds

children.content: South Australia's public breast cancer screening service conducted research without ethics approval or consent from almost 100 patients, prompting the service to "unreservedly" apologise.

image.altText: The office of BreastScreen SA in Adelaide.

items.title: Bandicoots bounce back in Brown Hill Creek as team works towards 'superhighway'

children.content: A small colony of endangered brown bandicoots just south of Adelaide continue to breed as conservationists hope to connect them with other populations.

image.altText: Southern brown bandicoot, Isoodon obesulus obesulus

items.title: Why Celine Cremer's family don't live with anger after remains found in forest

children.content: Backpacker Celine Cremer set off on a short walk with only a phone to guide her — and vanished. More than two years later, her family has an urgent message for other travellers.

items.title: The government is looking for cuts and thinks it has found its target

children.content: For the first time in this survey, more Australians are dissatisfied with the direction of the country than satisfied. The conflict between the US/Israel and Iran has delivered more than geopolitical uncertainty.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wears a dark suit jacket, white shirt and red tie. He has a serious facial expression.

children.content: Free public transport is a popular policy, but researchers say it's not enough to convince drivers to leave the car at home.

items.title: The Queensland region where locals avoid their own hospitals

children.content: In Queensland's Lockyer Valley, new figures suggest a third of the region's 41 hospital beds are not regularly available for new patients.

items.title: 'I had no-one to talk to': The 92yo trying to save his dying language

children.content: Peter is the last living speaker of the First Nations language Thiinma. When he met linguist Rosie, they used music to help keep his knowledge alive, and formed a beautiful friendship along the way.

items.title: Man faces Melbourne court after pedestrian death at comic convention

children.content: Awer Dau, 33, faces the Melbourne Magistrates' Court on Sunday evening, charged with culpable driving causing death. He allegedly hit a 20-year-old man while driving outside the Supanova Comic Con and Gaming convention on Saturday.

items.title: How these children turned a struggle with school into ambitious art show

items.title: Couple says loophole allowing theft from cars shipped interstate

items.title: Police investigate separate alleged murders near Newcastle

items.title: Could Australia make enough biofuel to keep us flying?

children.content: Australia exports billions of dollars worth of canola seed and corn husks to make sustainable jet fuel that is sold back to us. This is what it will take to make it here.
